records.ety: [L. expendere, expensum, to weigh out, pay out, lay out, lay out; ex out + pendere to weigh. See Poise, and cf. Spend.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Expended; p. pr. & vb. n. Expending.]

defs: To lay out, apply, or employ in any way; to consume by use; to use up or distribute, either in payment or in donations; to spend; as, they expend money for food or in charity; to expend time labor, and thought; to expend hay in feeding cattle, oil in a lamp, water in mechanical operations.
quotes: If my death might make this island happy . . . I would expend it with all willingness.

defs: To be laid out, used, or consumed.
To pay out or disburse money.
quotes: They go elsewhere to enjoy and to expend.
